headings: h3: I was born and grew up in Coventry.
h3: I want Coventry’s citizens to have chances they deserve.
h3: Help me fight for Coventry by supporting the Labour Party.

2026 All Aboard for Rail Reform Feb 1, 2026 Feb 1, 2026 VIEW ALL VIEW ALL My Priorities for Coventry Supporting our NHS I know how much people in Coventry need our NHS to work better. I have discussed people’s concerns at the City of Coventry Health Centre. I am proud to work for a Labour Government which quickly reached an agreement on junior doctors pay, and with a Chancellor who has announced a record-breaking £22.6 billion increase in day-to-day spending to deal with the NHS backlog which grew under the Tories. A safer city I want to tackle crime in Coventry East, and have discussed how we can best combat increasing numbers of burglaries as a priority. I’ve spoken to the Police Federation, the Chief Superintendent for the city, and the Chief Constable of West Midlands Police to focus on tackling crime. If you have any local crime concern

pages.title: About | From Coventry, For Coventry — Mary Creagh CBE Labour MP
headings: h2: Sign up for my email newsletter below
pages.text_excerpt: About | From Coventry, For Coventry — Mary Creagh CBE Labour MP 0 Skip to Content Mary Creagh CBE Labour MP About News Contact Willenhall Pride in Place Survey Subscribe To My Newsletter Open Menu Close Menu Mary Creagh CBE Labour MP About News Contact Willenhall Pride in Place Survey Subscribe To My Newsletter Open Menu Close Menu About News Contact Willenhall Pride in Place Survey Subscribe To My Newsletter About Me Coventry made me. I was born in Walsgrave hospital, after my parents settled here from Ireland. I grew up and went to school in the city under the Tories and Margaret Thatcher. Despite those hard times for our city, my teachers helped me win a place at Oxford. This led me to a job in Brussels, followed by teaching at Cranfield University. People in Coventry opened the door to opportunity for me which led to a life of public service. That’s why I’m determined to work hard for the people of my home city. I want everyone to get the chances that they deserve to thrive. I was elected as Member of Parliament for Coventry East in July 2024. I’m honoured to serve in the Labour government as Minister for Nature, and to lead Labour’s work on forests and on the circular economy. Mary Creagh CBE Labour MP for Coventry East Minister for Nature Sign up for my email newsletter below First Name Last Name Email Address Yes please! Thank you! Privacy Policy Privacy Policy

pages.text_excerpt: Willenhall Pride in Place Survey — Mary Creagh CBE Labour MP 0 Skip to Content Mary Creagh CBE Labour MP About News Contact Willenhall Pride in Place Survey Subscribe To My Newsletter Open Menu Close Menu Mary Creagh CBE Labour MP About News Contact Willenhall Pride in Place Survey Subscribe To My Newsletter Open Menu Close Menu About News Contact Willenhall Pride in Place Survey Subscribe To My Newsletter Willenhall Survey Pride in Place “I worked hard to secure £20 million for Willenhall. Now you get to decide how it’s spent. Please take a few minutes to fill out this survey. I want to know how you’d make Willenhall better.” - Mary Creagh CBE MP. Please only complete this survey if you live in the Willenhall area. If you live or work in Willenhall, you can apply to join the Neighbourhood Board to help deliver this opportunity. Please complete the last box of the survey to find out how.
